description: Technology choices, tradeoffs, and rationale from ADR stages

entries.title: PostgreSQL 15 over MongoDB for loyalty data
entries.content: Loyalty system requires ACID transactions (point awards, redemptions), complex joins (tier calculations across config+client+transaction), and referential integrity (cascade deletes for GDPR). PostgreSQL 15 chosen for: JSONB for flexible fields, UUID extension, strong constraint support, proven at scale for financial-adjacent data.

entries.title: Redis/Upstash for caching and rate limiting
entries.content: Redis chosen for: session cache, rate limit counters (sliding window), tier calculation cache. Upstash (serverless Redis) for cost efficiency at low-to-medium traffic. Can switch to ElastiCache if traffic exceeds Upstash free tier.

entries.title: ECS Fargate over Lambda for API + Worker
entries.content: ECS Fargate chosen over Lambda for: predictable latency (no cold starts for API), long-running SQS consumers (worker polls 20s), WebSocket support if needed later, Docker-based local dev parity. Lambda considered for individual event handlers but rejected due to 15min timeout and cold start impact on SLA.

entries.title: NestJS over Express for backend framework
entries.content: NestJS chosen for: built-in DI, module system (maps to domain-driven design), decorator-based guards/pipes/interceptors, TypeORM/Prisma integration, OpenAPI generation. Express too low-level for a 32-endpoint system with RBAC, validation, and multiple service layers.

entries.title: Next.js 14 PWA + CRM Dashboard dual frontend
entries.content: Two frontend surfaces: (1) PWA for end-customers (loyalty card, points, redemptions) — lightweight, mobile-first, magic-link auth. (2) CRM Dashboard for salon owners/staff — full CRUD, analytics, config wizard. Both share TypeScript types from Backend shared/ directory.

entries.title: Zod validation mandatory for all API route inputs
entries.content: Every new API route must validate with Zod schema. Pattern: const InputSchema = z.object({...}), parse with .safeParse(), return 400 on invalid. Not optional. Rationale: type safety, clear error messages, consistent DX across codebase.

entries.title: Agent Hub proxy + cached fallback pattern for all external calls
entries.content: All Agent Hub calls: (1) try with timeout, (2) on failure use cached fallback from data/ or return null, (3) never block pipeline on external timeout. Applied to: /api/chat, /api/agent-hub/*, KB sync. Decision: mandatory for any new Agent Hub integration.

entries.title: Tailwind CSS 4 CSS-first config — no tailwind.config.ts
entries.content: All CSS variables and design tokens defined in app/globals.css, not tailwind.config.ts. Tailwind 4 uses CSS-first configuration. All agents must follow this — Designer outputs globals.css, Frontend consumes it.

entries.title: TypeScript strict mode — noImplicitAny: true mandatory
entries.content: All new tsconfig.json must have noImplicitAny: true. Backend test-steps flagged noImplicitAny: false as code quality concern. Exception: don't change existing mid-sprint. Enforce at merge gate.

entries.title: Database migrations must be sequentially numbered with idempotent tracking
entries.content: Pattern: 001_create_xxx.sql, 002_add_yyy.sql. Must track applied migrations in migration_log table. No implicit 'run all SQL'. Always enforce sequence + idempotency. NestJS: TypeORM or custom runner with tracking.

entries.title: Agent tools two-layer architecture
entries.content:
## Two Tool Layers (as of 2026-03-21)

Pipeline executor uses MC local tools (callAI/callAIWithTools).
Agent Hub actions (Jira, Perplexity, workspace) are NOT available in pipeline.

### MC Executor Tools (used in pipeline)
- read_file, edit_file, create_file, list_files, run_command, save_failure_pattern

### Agent Hub Actions (used in chat only, unused in pipeline)
- Jira: 29 actions (createTicket, fetchTickets, setStoryPoints, etc.)
- Perplexity: web search
- Workspace: store/retrieve/vector

entries.title: MC vs SB comparison table
entries.content:
## Mission Control vs Singularity Bridge — Comparison (2026-03-21)

### Identity
- **Mission Control**: Pipeline orchestration — 13-stage workflow with quality gates, self-healing, structured contracts
- **Singularity Bridge**: Agent chat platform — rich chat/voice/screen share UI, 28+ integrations, multi-tenant

### Tech Stack Differences
- MC: Next.js 15.5 (unified), file-based storage, single user, Tailwind 4
- SB: Express + React/Vite (separate), MongoDB, JWT auth, multi-tenant, Tailwind 3.4

| S3.1 | Architect (ADR) | S

entries.title: Dev server launch: use node_modules/.bin/next directly, not npm run dev
entries.content: In pnpm monorepo on Windows, npm run dev fails because pnpm junction symlinks are broken. Working solution: run binary directly via node_modules/.bin/next dev -p {port}. This bypasses npm script resolution and pnpm symlink chain. Example: cd apps/secretutka && node_modules/.bin/next dev -p 3013. Always specify -p flag explicitly. PORT env var alone is not reliable across Next.js versions.

entries.title: npm install in pnpm monorepo: move root package.json temporarily
entries.content: To npm install in any project inside pnpm monorepo on Windows: (1) mv root/package.json root/package.json.bak, (2) mv root/pnpm-workspace.yaml root/pnpm-workspace.yaml.bak, (3) cd apps/project && rm -rf node_modules && npm install --ignore-scripts, (4) restore both files. This bypasses pnpm workspace detection and creates real files instead of broken junctions. All 13 projects work after this. Script: scripts/setup-projects.mjs should use this method.

entries.title: MC product packaging plan
entries.content:
## MC → Product Plan (parked, not active yet)

8 blockers identified before MC can become a product:
1. Auth (login/signup, roles)
2. Storage (JSON files → DB)
3. Multi-user (teams, permissions)
4. API keys (user-managed, encrypted)
5. Onboarding (setup wizard, demo pipeline)
6. Deploy (Vercel/Docker/VPS)
7. Landing page + docs
8. Billing (usage-based or subscription)

**Current priority:** NOT product packaging. First — make Projects page functional so real projects can be launched and managed

entries.title: MC unified product architecture
entries.content:
## Vision (from Michael, 2026-03-25)

Projects page is the **central hub** of MC. Everything flows from it:

1. **Activate** project → it appears in Orchestrator, Chat, Analytics, etc.
2. **Import** new project → scan apps/ directory or clone from git
3. **Preview** project → local dev server on assigned port
4. **Orchestrate** → pipeline runs are tied to project, not standalone
5. **Sync across pages** → agents/teams/costs/analytics/KB all scoped by active project

### Flow
```
Projects page →

entries.title: Session 2026-03-25 — MC cognitive architecture + projects
entries.content:
## What was built

### Cognitive Architecture
- KB: 100+ entries, 5 categories, SHA-256 hash validation
- Stage Contracts: 8 deterministic I/O contracts + dynamic KB adaptation
- Preflight Simulation: predicts pipeline from 114 runs + KB
- S0.2 Strategy Architect: iterative replan (44% → 64%)
- Agent KB Self-Awareness: agents read their own history
- Simulation → Auto-routing: mode upgrades, guard agents
- Smart stage filtering: skip irrelevant agents per task type

### Infrastructure
- Real-tim

entries.title: Intent classifier needs tuning � add/toggle/feature tasks should go to pipeline
entries.content: Current intent classifier sends "add dark mode toggle" to direct mode (0.9 confidence). This is too complex for direct � needs state management, localStorage, theme provider. Rule: "add" + "toggle/switch/feature/module" ? pipeline, not direct. Direct should only handle: rename, fix color, change text, swap values. Anything that creates new functionality ? pipeline.

entries.title: Claude in MC UI — Command Bar architecture
entries.content:
## Architecture: Claude as Tool in MC UI

### Single entry point: Command Bar
User types anything → Intent classifier → execution mode:
- **DIRECT**: simple edits (<3 files) → Claude API + tools
- **PIPELINE**: multi-stage, security, architecture → triggers pipeline
- **HYBRID**: Claude prepares context + quick fixes, pipeline does heavy lifting

### Claude Layer constraints (CRITICAL)
Claude MUST:
- Read KB before acting
- Respect project context (active project selector)
- Log everything to or

entries.title: Phase 2 plan — Global Project Selector + page unification
entries.content:
## Phase 2 — Approved Plan (2026-03-26)

### Core: Global Project Selector
- Topbar dropdown — single source of truth for activeProjectId
- app-store.ts owns activeProjectId, orchestration-store subscribes
- All project-scoped pages react to changes

### Page Classification

**Project-scoped (sync with selector):**
- /orchestration (tabs: Pipeline, Contracts, Analytics)
- /jira (replace local selector)
- /knowledge (project layer + global layer)
- /chat (agent list global, but chat window shows

entries.title: UI design system: light theme with indigo/slate/emerald palette
entries.content: Home page redesigned for product-ready look. Colors: indigo-600 primary, slate-900 headings, slate-600 body, emerald/amber/rose for status. No cyan/purple glow on light theme. White cards with slate-200 borders. Font: system (no Rajdhani), mono for data. Spacing: gap-4, p-4 cards. Reference: Linear, Vercel dashboard, Notion sidebar. No bg-black, no animate-pulse, no radial gradients.
